title: AI Content Creation: Drafting Posts & Carousels

questions.question: What is the Voice-First Drafting Workflow?

options.text: Using voice dictation to write posts
Training AI on 10-20 of your best posts as style samples BEFORE asking it to generate new ones, so it drafts in YOUR voice, not corporate-speak

questions.explanation: The Voice-First Workflow means feeding AI 10-20 of your best-performing posts as style samples before generating new ones. The AI learns your sentence rhythm, vocabulary, and tone, then drafts in your voice instead of its default corporate-speak. Most people skip this and get generic output.

questions.question: How many LinkedIn posts can you generate in 60 minutes using the AI-assisted workflow?
questions.explanation: The lesson promises a repeatable system to generate 5-10 LinkedIn posts in 60 minutes, in your voice, that move prospects toward your offer. This is possible because AI eliminates the 80% of content creation that's friction — blank page, structure decisions, and 'how do I start' paralysis.

questions.question: What are the four steps of the Voice Training Protocol?

options.text: Collect your best posts (10-20 samples), Identify your voice patterns, Create a voice guide document, Test with a calibration batch
Choose a topic, Ask AI to write, Copy-paste, Post
Research competitors, Copy their style, Adapt slightly, Post daily

questions.explanation: The four steps are: collect 10-20 of your best posts by engagement, identify your voice patterns (sentence length, vocabulary, tone, structure, hooks), create a voice guide document for AI reference, and test with a calibration batch to refine. This prevents the generic AI content that plagues most LinkedIn profiles.

questions.question: Asking ChatGPT to 'write a LinkedIn post about positioning' without voice training will produce content that sounds like your authentic voice.
questions.explanation: False. Without voice training, AI defaults to generic corporate-speak that sounds like every other AI-generated post. The lesson calls this 'using AI backwards.' The pros train AI on their voice first, then generate content that maintains their authentic tone and style.

questions.question: Find your top 5 LinkedIn posts by engagement. What patterns do you notice in sentence length, vocabulary, tone, structure, and hooks? Write a 3-sentence 'voice guide' that you could give to an AI to help it write in your style.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response identifies specific patterns from their actual posts (not theoretical), creates a concrete voice guide with 3+ distinct characteristics (sentence length, tone, vocabulary preferences), and demonstrates understanding that voice consistency is what makes AI-generated content feel authentic.
